name: Browserless (Headless Chrome API)
what_it_does: Hosted headless Chrome service with REST API and WebSocket connections for Puppeteer/Playwright, enabling PDF generation, screenshots, web scraping, and browser automation without managing Chrome infrastructure.
use_cases: HTML-to-PDF generation for invoices, reports, and documents requiring pixel-perfect CSS rendering
Full-page screenshots for visual testing or content previews in automated pipelines
Web scraping JavaScript-heavy sites that require browser rendering
Browser automation for testing workflows via Puppeteer or Playwright over WebSocket
Content extraction from dynamic SPAs that API-only scrapers cannot handle
not_for: Simple HTML-to-PDF without JavaScript (use DocRaptor or WeasyPrint for simpler use cases)
Very high-concurrency PDF generation at low cost (browser rendering is resource-intensive)
Static site scraping where simpler HTTP clients suffice
Applications requiring browser state persistence across many sessions
best_when: You need Puppeteer/Playwright-compatible headless Chrome as a managed service, especially for PDF generation that must faithfully render complex CSS and JavaScript.
avoid_when: Your PDF needs are template-based and don't require full browser rendering, or you're budget-sensitive about per-session costs.

auth.notes: API token as query parameter for REST endpoints or as part of WebSocket URL. Single token per account. Token grants full access to the account's browser sessions.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier: limited concurrent sessions per month
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $40/month (6 concurrent sessions); Scale from $150/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Pricing based on concurrent browser sessions, not per-PDF or per-request. Good for predictable workloads; expensive for bursty use. Self-hosting Browserless OSS is free.

performance.rate_limits: Limited by concurrent session count per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: WebSocket errors surface as Puppeteer/Playwright exceptions in the calling code. REST API errors are standard HTTP codes. Timeout errors are clear but require tuning per use case.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Each browser session is a unique execution. PDF generation must be retried from scratch on failure.
known_agent_gotchas: Concurrent session limit is the key constraint — exceeding it queues or rejects requests
WebSocket connections for Puppeteer/Playwright have connection timeouts — implement reconnection logic
Navigation timeout for pages with slow resources must be configured; default may be too short
Memory leaks in browser sessions can exhaust session pool — ensure pages and browsers are properly closed
Site-specific anti-bot measures may block browserless IPs — IP rotation or residential proxies may be needed
PDF rendering of complex pages may differ from local Chrome — test edge cases carefully
API token in WebSocket URL — use secure connection and rotate tokens periodically
